_canonical_source: docs/AUDIENCE_BRIEF.md — update the brief first, then re-render this file. If they drift, the brief wins.
participant_profile: Approximately 750 senior business professionals, founders, designers, and creatives. Predominantly European, 40+ countries, skewing 35-55. Self-selected at ~€1,895 entry. Philosophically literate romantic reformists committed to transforming capitalism from within — embedded in corporate systems (SAP, Google, BCG, Porsche, Chanel represented) while critiquing them. Comfortable with interdisciplinary vocabulary, foreign-language terminology, and emotional openness unusual for a business audience. Clustered across seven identifiable intellectual factions (cognitive-sovereignty hawks, sovereign-AI builders, posthumanist reframers, anti-capitalist/collapse-aware economists, democratic institutionalists, re-enchantment/spiritual, geopolitics realists). Activate on genuine intellectual risk and visible contradiction; go flat on standard progressive talking points without edge. The program's marketing foregrounds beauty, care, and regeneration, but its actual center of gravity is agency — who acts, who decides, what happens when machines share or seize those functions. Their deepest vulnerability: their well-curated openness is itself the failure mode — they are too good at performing reception to know when they are not actually being changed.
